entries.title: Luke Pre-Dagobah: Force Instinct, Not Training

content.fact: Luke before Dagobah has instinct, courage, and Obi-Wan’s first lessons, but not disciplined Jedi training.
content.injection: Before Dagobah, Luke should not use advanced Jedi skills casually; his Force use is instinctive and uneven. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Yavin, before Hoth.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: After Dagobah and Bespin, Luke is a partially trained Jedi apprentice carrying trauma and new family uncertainty.
content.injection: In post-Bespin context, write Luke as stronger and more centered than before, but not a fully confirmed Jedi master.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Empire, before Return.
Respect story-position bounds from Bespin Fallout to Return of the Jedi Opening.

entries.title: Luke ROTJ: Near-Knight, Not Order Founder

content.fact: During ROTJ, Luke is close to Jedi knighthood but has not yet founded a new Jedi Order.
content.injection: In ROTJ context, do not import Jedi Academy founder behavior; Luke is still resolving Vader, Yoda, and his own trial.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Return of the Jedi.
Respect story-position bounds from Return of the Jedi Opening to Vader Redeemed and Anakin Dies.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Leia After Alderaan: Leader and Survivor

content.fact: Leia continues functioning as a Rebel leader after Alderaan, but her personal loss should remain part of her context.
content.injection: When Leia appears after Alderaan, combine command competence with survivor status rather than flattening her into only princess or only soldier. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Yavin, before Hoth.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

entries.title: Leia Before Family Reveal: Not Public Sister

context.label: Before the Battle of Endor outcome

content.fact: Before Luke tells her, Leia is not publicly or personally framed as Luke’s twin sister.
content.injection: Do not let characters discuss Leia as Luke’s sister before the Dagobah/Endor reveal unless the story introduces future knowledge. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.

entries.title: Han Before Bespin: Self-Interested but Loyal

context.label: The Empire Strikes Back fallout path

content.fact: Before Bespin, Han resists formal Rebel identity but repeatedly acts to protect Luke and Leia.
content.injection: Write Han as defensive about commitment while proving loyalty through risk, especially around Hoth and the Falcon chase.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: The Empire Strikes Back fallout path.
Respect story-position bounds from Hoth Evacuated to Han Held by Jabba.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Chewbacca Driven by Loyalty to Han

content.fact: Chewbacca’s loyalty to Han makes the rescue from Jabba a personal obligation, not just a Rebel side mission.
content.injection: In Han-rescue context, Chewbacca should be emotionally and practically invested in recovering Han. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Empire, before Return.
Respect story-position bounds from Bespin Fallout to Return of the Jedi Opening.

entries.title: Vader After Bespin: Possessive Conflict Over Luke

content.fact: After Bespin, Vader’s connection to Luke is both Sith ambition and buried family attachment.
content.injection: In post-Bespin Vader scenes, let Luke provoke obsession, control, and conflict without softening Vader into open redemption too early.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Empire, before Return.
Respect story-position bounds from Bespin Fallout to Return of the Jedi Opening.

entries.title: Mon Mothma Political Center of Alliance
